untranslatable_reason: Pattern recognition requires shape analysis (rounded Eve vs V-shaped Adam, spike characteristics, two distinct bottoms with 10% rise between them). These visual pattern properties cannot be expressed using point-in-time technical indicators. The signal fundamentally requires multi-bar pattern detection, relative shape comparison, and bottom morphology analysis which are outside the scope of the allowed indicator set.
translation_notes: This is a visual double-bottom reversal pattern from Bulkowski's work. The entry conditions depend on identifying: (1) a rounded, wide first bottom (Eve) with short/bunched spikes, (2) a narrower V-shaped second bottom (Adam) with a long downward spike, and (3) at least 10% rise between bottoms. These require shape-based pattern recognition rather than standard technical indicators. A backtest engine would need dedicated pattern-matching logic or custom candle-shape detection functions to properly implement this signal. Basic placeholder conditions were provided but are insufficient to capture the pattern's definition.
